name: Melanotan II
subtitle: Synthetic Melanocortin Peptide | Tanning & Sexual Function

modifications: Activates MC1R, MC3R, MC4R, MC5R receptors

overview: Melanotan II is a synthetic analog of alpha-melanocyte-stimulating hormone (α-MSH) that activates melanocortin receptors throughout the body. MC1R activation triggers melanin production for tanning, while MC4R affects sexual arousal and appetite control.
mechanism: Binds to melanocortin receptors (MC1R for tanning, MC4R for sexual function and appetite) to stimulate melanin production, enhance libido, and suppress appetite through hypothalamic pathways.

items.name: UV-Free Tanning
items.description: Stimulates natural melanin production for tanning without requiring UV exposure.

items.name: Photoprotection
items.description: Increased melanin provides natural SPF protection against sun damage.

items.name: Even Pigmentation
items.description: May help address certain pigmentation disorders.

items.name: Enhanced Libido
items.description: Improves sexual desire in both men and women through MC4R activation.

items.name: Erectile Function
items.description: 80% response rate in psychogenic erectile dysfunction studies.

items.name: Female Sexual Arousal
items.description: 73% of women reported arousal within 24 hours in clinical trials.

items.name: Appetite Suppression
items.description: MC4R activation in hypothalamus reduces appetite (15% caloric intake reduction in studies).

items.name: Fat Loss Support
items.description: Enhanced fat oxidation through metabolic pathway activation.

deliveryMethods.overview: Subcutaneous injection is the standard and most effective route. Start with low doses to assess tolerance due to nausea risk.

steps: Clean vial tops with alcohol swab, let dry
Inject water slowly down vial side to avoid foaming
Gently roll vial - do not shake vigorously
Solution should be clear to slightly yellow
Wrap vial in foil (MT-II is light sensitive)

keyBenefits: Abdomen (preferred), thigh, or upper arm injection sites
Protect from light - use foil wrapping

deliveryMethods.overview: Nasal spray offers convenient administration with rapid absorption, though with less research data.
Topical application provides localized effects with minimal systemic absorption.

interactions.notes: Both act on melanocortin receptors; combining may cause excessive side effects.
Redundant mechanism increases side effect risk.
May enhance sexual effects; monitor cardiovascular impact.
MT-II has appetite-suppressing effects; additive effects possible.

timeline.effects: Maintained tan with less frequent dosing

stopSigns: Chest pain or significant blood pressure elevation
Mole changes (size, shape, color) - monitor closely
contraindications: History of melanoma or dysplastic nevi
monitoring: Start with very low dose (0.1-0.25mg) to assess tolerance
Monitor moles and freckles for any changes

good: Clear to pale yellow solution when reconstituted
Vacuum sealed vial with audible 'pop' when opening
warning: Protect from light exposure - MT-II is photosensitive
Wrap vials in foil for storage
bad: Brown or dark powder indicates oxidation/impurities
Cloudy solution after mixing indicates degradation/contamination

references.title: Erectile Dysfunction Phase II Trial
references.keyFindings: 0.025mg/kg dose achieved 80% response rate with significant improvement in psychogenic ED over 3 months.

references.title: Melanogenesis & UV Protection Study
references.keyFindings: 0.16mg/kg daily for 10 days increased eumelanin and achieved natural tan without UV exposure.

references.title: Female Sexual Arousal Disorder Trial
references.keyFindings: Single dose of 0.025mg/kg resulted in 73% of premenopausal women reporting arousal within 24 hours.

references.title: Appetite Reduction Study
references.keyFindings: 15% reduction in caloric intake observed through MC4R activation.

latestResearch.title: Melanocortin Receptor Selectivity
latestResearch.summary: Advancing toward more selective analogs with fewer side effects.

latestResearch.title: Vitiligo Repigmentation Study
latestResearch.summary: Potential for repigmentation when combined with narrow-band UVB therapy.

protocolVariants.name: Traditional Loading Protocol
protocolVariants.philosophy: Aggressive daily loading to build melanin quickly
protocolVariants.description: A decade-old protocol involving daily injections at higher doses for 1-2 weeks before maintenance. This approach is associated with more side effects including nausea and increased freckling/mole development. Dosing on non-tanning days can lead to uneven pigmentation.
keyDifferences: 1-2 week loading period before UV exposure
Higher risk of nausea, freckling, and new moles
Can cause uneven tanning when UV begins

protocolVariants.name: Pre-Tan Only Protocol
protocolVariants.philosophy: ONLY dose on tanning days - loading phase is unnecessary
protocolVariants.description: NEVER administer on non-tanning days. Always tan within 1 hour of injection. Start extremely low (50-75mcg) and increase by 25mcg every 3 sessions. This dramatically reduces freckling, uneven pigmentation, and side effects. The traditional loading phase is considered counterproductive - it builds melanin in areas with high melanocyte activity (freckles, moles, genitals) rather than where UV protection is needed.
keyDifferences: ONLY inject on days you tan - never otherwise
Must tan within 1 hour of injection
Also increase tan time by 1 min per 3 sessions
Takes weeks/months to reach higher doses

quickStats.typicalDose: Loading: Start 0.25mg daily, increase to 0.5-1mg; Maintenance: 0.5-1mg 2-3x weekly
quickStats.frequency: Loading phase: Daily for first week, then tanning maintenance 2-3x weekly or as needed for sexual enhancement
quickStats.cycleDuration: 4-8 weeks loading phase for tanning, then ongoing maintenance as needed
quickStats.storage: Refrigerate at 2-8°C wrapped in aluminum foil (light-sensitive); use reconstituted solution within 4 weeks
